Last Light armed for the next run.

feedback:
Strengths
- The board-first HUD reads quickly in motion. Health, threat, and objective framing stay visible without burying the board.
- Town service screens are easy to parse once opened. The bank/meta layer is especially clear about what gold can do beyond shopping.
- The floor objective gate gives the first dungeon level a stronger purpose than a simple stair rush.
- Objective automation covered 4/4 current depth-1 objective types in repeat runs.
- Meta review: 98% of tracked runs started with a contract, and bank persistence was revisited after 1/1 successful returns.
- First-objective pacing averages: clear turn 40, searches before clear 0.5.

Friction
- The first-time flow is improved, but it still leans on automation to brute-force objective variants and room states. The title and town guidance are now legible enough that the harness can follow the keep route directly without a full-floor reveal.
- The live feed is now visible in the main HUD, but it still needs repeated phone checks to confirm the extra lines never crowd the board.
- The first search revealed 12 additional route points without fully revealing the floor.
- Placement stress: 0 no-path cases and 0 touching-room cases across 60 depth-1 starts.
- The first objective (Recover The Relic) resolved without needing a full map reveal. Coverage for the other depth-1 variants now uses the same no-reveal objective pass.

Run Notes
- Objective on depth 1: Recover The Relic
- Final state: The valley town below the keep, turn 159, HP 56/56
- Search probe ran before objective completion to verify route extension without full-floor reveal.
- Review scenarios captured: first-descent, first-visible-threat, spell-blast-preview, extract-or-greed, town-return-bank.
